metadata.title: Strategic reading lenses — Sun Tzu's Art of War, the OSS/CIA Simple Sabotage Field Manual, and Confucius's Analects (esp. 12.7 & 13.3) — and the 'let rivals rise to erode the incumbent' long game
metadata.frame: Three classical/strategic texts, read at depth (not the famous one-liners), used as interpretive LENSES over the whole corpus. They converge on one idea the project already circles: the decisive variable in a contested system is not arms or money but LEGITIMACY / TRUST, and the cheapest way to win is to erode the rival's cohesion, alliances, and strategy rather than fight its army. Texts are verified before use; translations (esp. of the Analects, incl. Japanese readings) vary and that is flagged. These are lenses and graded INTERPRETATION, not claims of proven coordinated intent by any actor.
metadata.relevance: Reframes [[spec-cross-border-settlement-rails]] (eroding dollar entrenchment without battle), [[spec-manufactured-division]] (sabotage of cohesion/trust), [[macro-official-data-integrity]] + [[digitalid-orchestration-real-incentive]] (contests over names and trust), and [[spec-foreign-influence-comparison]] (doomed-spies / disinformation). Companion catalog of re-read tasks accompanies this block.
metadata.sources: Sun Tzu (suntzusaid.com / Giles translation; Columbia text), OSS Simple Sabotage Field Manual 1944 (Project Gutenberg #26184 / archive.org; CIA-declassified 2008), Analects (12.7, 13.3, 2.3, 15.24; Legge/Lau/Slingerland + Chinese source text). Full URLs in the rendered .md.

lens_sun_tzu.summary: Read past 'win without fighting': the Art of War is a theory of legitimacy, economy, position, and information.

points.item: The Five Factors (五事) start with the Way
points.detail: Sun Tzu opens by ranking war's determinants: 道 the Moral Law/'the Way' (whether the people are in complete accord with their ruler - i.e., legitimacy/cohesion), 天 Heaven (timing/conditions), 地 Earth (terrain/position), 將 the Commander (wisdom, sincerity, benevolence, courage, strictness), 法 Method/discipline (organization, logistics). LEGITIMACY is the FIRST factor - the same primacy Confucius gives trust.

Supreme: attack the enemy's STRATEGY; next, his ALLIANCES (split the coalition by diplomacy); next, his army; worst, besiege cities. Subdue without fighting. Applied: the dollar SYSTEM is the strategic center of gravity - attack the strategy and the alliances around it, not the 'army.'

'There is no instance of a country having benefited from prolonged warfare.' Protracted conflict drains the state THAT WAGES it - so delay/attrition cuts both ways, and forcing tempo can be the counter (relevant to the crypto-legislation delay in [[spec-crypto-legislation-forcing]]).

Victory rests on foreknowledge, got from people not omens; the five spies are local, inward (insiders), converted (doubled), DOOMED (carry deliberate disinformation), and surviving. 'Doomed spies' = planned disinformation - the ancestor of modern influence-ops/manufactured-division.

lens_simple_sabotage.summary: The OSS manual is not just the committee-meeting meme: it is a full doctrine of cheap, deniable degradation of an adversary's capacity to function and to communicate.

points.detail: Written 17 Jan 1944 by the Office of Strategic Services; declassified by the CIA in 2008; meant to train ordinary 'citizen-saboteurs' in occupied Europe.

It directs attacks on transport and especially on the means authorities use to transmit instructions and morale material - telephone, telegraph, power, radio, newspapers, placards, public notices. The information/communication layer is the prime target (cf. [[geopolitics-cables-space-layer]], [[spec-censorship-finance-adtech]]).

Insist everything go through 'channels'; refer matters to large committees 'for further study'; haggle over precise wordings; raise irrelevant issues; give lengthy incomprehensible explanations; misfile; spread disturbing rumors; lower morale and create confusion.

points.item: The deniable 'human element' is the weapon
points.detail: The manual's key insight: a second type of sabotage 'requires no destructive tools whatsoever' - faulty decisions, a non-cooperative attitude, inducing others to follow - and is 'frequently responsible for accidents, delays, and general obstruction even under normal conditions.' It is INDISTINGUISHABLE from ordinary dysfunction. Deniability is the point - and it means obstruction can be foreign-induced OR self-inflicted, and you usually cannot tell which.

lens_confucius_analects.summary: Beyond 12.7: the Analects is a theory of governance by legitimacy, in which correct LANGUAGE and exemplary virtue, not coercion, hold a polity together.

points.detail: Asked what to give up first among arms (兵), food (食), and the people's trust (信), Confucius keeps trust last: 民無信不立 - 'without the people's trust the state cannot stand.' Legitimacy is the irreducible base.

'If names are not correct, language is not in accordance with the truth of things; affairs cannot be carried to success; rites and music will not flourish; punishments will not be properly awarded; the people will not know where to put hand or foot.' Calling things by their true names is the precondition of a functioning state - and the antidote to propaganda. This IS the project's evidence-grading discipline: a surveillance system named 'safety,' control named 'inclusion,' or a lobby conflated with a people, are failures of zhengming.

'Lead the people with laws and order them with punishments, and they will avoid wrongdoing but have no sense of shame. Lead them with virtue (德) and order them with ritual (禮), and they will have a sense of shame and become good.' Coercion produces evasion; legitimacy produces self-correction - the critique that runs through the control-rail (digital-ID/CBDC/censorship) threads.

points.item: The web of virtues + translation caveat
points.detail: 仁 ren (benevolence), 禮 li (propriety/ritual), 義 yi (rightness), 信 xin (trust), 恕 shu (reciprocity - 15.24, 'do not impose on others what you do not desire'), embodied by the 君子 junzi. Translations differ materially (Legge vs Lau vs Slingerland; classical Chinese vs Japanese kanbun readings can shift nuance), so claims here cite the passage, not a single rendering.

the_long_game_thesis.summary: The interpretive synthesis: a patient challenger can benefit from helping MANY rivals/currencies rise - because each erodes the incumbent's entrenchment - before bidding for its own dominance later.

points.detail: Rather than confront the dollar/US system head-on (Sun Tzu's worst option), a long-game actor encourages a pluralized world - multiple currencies, settlement rails, and power centers. Each marginal alternative weakens the incumbent's network effects and coercive leverage (sanctions). Only once entrenchment is loosened does the challenger press its own bid. 'Let a hundred flowers bloom' as anti-hegemony, not generosity.

By all three lenses the incumbent's true center of gravity is LEGITIMACY/TRUST - of the dollar, of US institutions, of shared facts. Eroding trust (via data-integrity doubt, manufactured division, institutional dysfunction) is higher-leverage and cheaper than any military or monetary attack. Confucius 12.7 + Sun Tzu's 道 + the sabotage manual all point at the same soft target.

points.item: Cuts both ways / not one actor
points.detail: Crucially, the same dynamics are SELF-INFLICTABLE: institutional dysfunction, polarization, and eroded data-integrity degrade trust whether or not any foreign actor causes them - and the sabotage manual says you usually cannot tell the difference. The lens indicts a STATE OF AFFAIRS, not a single conspirator.

reading_the_corpus.summary: How the lenses re-read existing threads (a map for the re-read task catalog).

mappings.lens: Sun Tzu (attack strategy+alliances; economy of force) + long-game
mappings.reframe: Bypass rails erode the dollar's strategic center without battle; letting rivals rise is the patient play.

mappings.lens: Simple Sabotage (deniable human-element obstruction; rumor; morale) + Sun Tzu doomed-spies
mappings.reframe: Division and disinformation are the cheapest, most deniable attacks on cohesion - the irreducible 道/信.
Mislabeling (surveillance='safety', control='inclusion') and coercive control corrode legitimacy; precise naming is the defense.

mappings.lens: Confucius 12.7 (trust) + Sun Tzu economy of force
mappings.reframe: Self-marked value and circular funding are bets on continued TRUST; a forcing event is a legitimacy shock, not just a price move.

mappings.lens: Simple Sabotage (channels/committees/haggle-wording) + Sun Tzu Ch.2 (prolonged war drains)
mappings.reframe: The delay IS the sabotage playbook; the counter is to force tempo and rectify names (name the obstruction). See [[spec-crypto-legislation-forcing]].

checks.check: Sun Tzu five factors / Ch.2 / Ch.13
checks.result: 道天地將法; 'no country benefited from prolonged warfare'; five spies incl. doomed (disinformation) - verified vs suntzusaid.com/Giles + Columbia text.
OSS 1944, CIA-declassified 2008; physical (comms-targeting) + organizational + morale sections; 'no destructive tools... human element' deniability - verified vs Project Gutenberg #26184 / Wikipedia.
民無信不立 (trust last); 正名 rectification of names; 道之以德齊之以禮 (virtue over punishment) - verified vs Chinese source + Legge/Lau; translations noted as variable.

non_claims: These are interpretive LENSES and graded interpretation - NOT claims that any actor has proven, coordinated intent to execute Sun Tzu / the sabotage manual.
The long-game thesis applies to MULTIPLE actors and to SELF-INFLICTED dysfunction; it is not an accusation against a single state.
Text claims cite the passage, not one translation; Analects renderings vary (incl. Japanese kanbun) and that is flagged.
No edges from this analytical block enter the SCC / Z3 / TLA+ proofs.

edges.raw_instrument: long-game lens (Sun Tzu): enabling dollar alternatives erodes the incumbent's entrenchment without direct conflict
edges.note: OVERLAY: interpretive lens; excluded from proofs

edges.raw_instrument: Simple-Sabotage lens: deniable degradation of cohesion/communication is the cheapest attack on trust (道/信)
edges.note: OVERLAY: interpretive lens; excluded from proofs

limits: Three strategic texts read at depth (Sun Tzu's five factors / economy of force / five spies; the Simple Sabotage manual's communications-targeting + deniable human-element doctrine; the Analects' rectification-of-names and virtue-over-coercion, with trust as the irreducible base) used as interpretive lenses over the corpus, plus the 'let rivals rise to erode the incumbent, then bid later' long-game thesis (graded interpretation, multi-actor, self-inflictable). Texts verified before use; Analects translation variance flagged. Drives the re-read task catalog and the crypto-legislation-forcing block. Overlay edges excluded from the proofs. source: see verification_log and the rendered .md for full URLs.
